communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

As referenced in the article "Cadets Present Research Projects at SURF" By Marianne Hause and the interviews given by Mr. Jeremiah Woods to the Washington Post, I am requesting the following records:

1. Records relating to the selection of Mr. Jeremiah Woods for the SURF project and his research and coordination with the applicable faculty member for his research.

2.  The research paper and underlying references and source materials used for the paper, prepared by Jeremiah Woods ’27, titled “African Americans at VMI: 1851-1865.” 

The paper was presented publicly, and the project was publicised, so the records should be open and readily available without any delay. 

I am willing to accept incremental fulfillment of my request, starting with the research paper he presented, which I expect can be provided immediately.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Anonymous.
